Probability is expressed as a number ranging from 0 to 1.
0 is never
1 is always.

What is the probability of obtaining a recessive phenotype in a monohybrid cross?

Sum Rule
The probability of either one of two independent (mutually exclusive) events is the sum of their individual probabilities.
OR

The number of times an event is expected to happen
The number of opportunities for an event to happen
Probability =
What is the probability of obtaining a homozygous dominant or recessive phenotype in a monohybrid cross?

Product Rule
The probability that independent events will occur at the same time is the product of their respective probabilities.
And
What is the probability of having first a boy and then a girl?
What is the probability of having first two boys, and then two girls?

What is the probability of having first two boys, and then two girls?

If a couple have 10 girls in a row, what is the chance that the next child will be a girl?

If a couple have 10 girls in a row, what is the chance that the next child will be a girl?
1/2
Don’t be fooled by problems like this.
Events that have already happened don’t change the probability of future events.

Pedigree, and symbol explanation
Most mutations are recessive

Most pedigrees show a recessive pattern of inheritance
Horizontal inheritance
Vertical inheritance is suggestive of Dominant mutations

Two parts, what’s the chance they have the allele?
What’s the chance they will pass it on?

Huntingon’s disease is a rare fatal, degenerative neurological disease in which individuals start to show symptoms, on average, in their 40’s.  It is caused by a dominant allele.  Joe, a man in his 20s, just learned that his father has Huntington’s disease.
What is the probability that Joe will also develop the disease?
Given the information presented in the question above, Joe and his wife have been eager to start a family.  What is the probability that their first child will eventually develop the disease?

½         Hint: “rare” means you can assume an unknown is homozygous normal.
